analysis_description: Round 3 tapes days 0–2: Black–Scholes implied vol vs m_t=log(K/S)/sqrt(T) with T from round3description DTE mapping (CSV day 0→8d at open, 1→7, 2→6) plus intraday winding per plot_iv_smile_round3 (dte_effective). Vega per strike from BS vega(S,K,T,iv). Top-of-book spread width on extract and each VEV from bid_price_1/ask_price_1.
used_to_decide: Median extract spread ~5; deep OTM vouchers (e.g. VEV_4000) median spread ~21 vs ATM ~3–4 — motivates wider minimum quote width and optional skip/wing filter when edge is small. Vega-weighted ATM IV ~0.241 vs global median ~0.245; right-wing IV median higher than left — keep quadratic smile fit, update slowly with EWMA so fair does not chase tick noise.

analysis_description: Offline parameter sweep: EWMA alpha on vega-weighted polyfit coeff path (day 0, timestamp step 200). Mean L2 step size of (c2,c1,c0) vector vs alpha.
used_to_decide: Chose alpha=0.045 as balance between responsiveness and smoothness (between 0.04 and 0.06 in grid); paired with removing extract-only MM after v0 showed large extract losses.

analysis_description: Cross-check: v2 wing filter thresholds tied to iteration-0 spread medians by strike bucket; micro re-backtest vs v1.
used_to_decide: Small PnL gain vs v1 (+23 total); kept v2 as slightly better and simpler than a full new branch — documents that wing filter mainly helps day 1 VEV_4000.

analysis_description: IV-fair edge threshold study across days 0-2: per timestamp fit vega-weighted quadratic IV smile, compute abs(mid-fair) and half-spread; evaluate candidate take thresholds.
used_to_decide: At abs-edge>=2.0, ~98% of samples are in half-spread<=2 books, suggesting a plausible selective-taker opportunity. Implemented in v3 but backtest falsified the improvement hypothesis (large losses), so taker escalation is rejected for now.

analysis_description: Post-mortem of v3 losses by strike/day from backtest output.
used_to_decide: Losses concentrated in near-ATM strikes (especially VEV_5100/5000) on days 1-2 indicate overreaction to transient edge. Rolled back to conservative/no-taker variants.

analysis_description: Small parameter sweep around wing guard (EDGE_MULT_WIDE 0.85 -> 1.0) while preserving sticky-smile core.
used_to_decide: No aggregate gain vs v2 (exact tie). Tie-break by simplicity keeps v2 as preferred parent for next iteration.

analysis_description: VEV smile curvature stability vs extract EWMA rate: for each alpha, subsample (step 100) per day 0-2, fit vega-weighted quad IV in m_t with S = EWMA of extract mid, record std of poly coeff c2 (curvature) over time. Same BS IV/vega machinery as live trader.
used_to_decide: Grid selected alpha=0.045 to minimize c2 jiggle; implemented as trader_v6. Backtest: total PnL 19246 vs 19255 for v2, so the smoother anchor did not improve realized PnL here; next iterations should stay on tick-S smile (v2) unless a different smoothing hook is found.

analysis_description: Grid over smile-coeff EMA rate alpha in {0.02,0.03,0.045,0.06} on days 0-2, step 25: for each path, sequential EMA of poly coeffs from per-tick vega-weighted fit; at VEV_5200 record median |Delta fair| between steps and |BS call theta| (dV/dT, r=0) at sticky IV. Fair uses S from tape, T from dte_effective/365 (round3 DTE + intraday wind).
used_to_decide: Median |Delta fair| for VEV_5200 is ~2.0 and nearly flat in alpha in this grid; |theta| is huge (~1785/year) as expected for short T—dominant signal is moneyness/IV, not a small EMA tweak. Tried trader_v7 with EMA 0.03: backtest tied v2 at 19255; default remains v2 (0.045) for fewer moving parts unless future work needs slower reaction.

analysis_description: Round 3 tapes days 0-2, step 200: implied IV from mid per strike, then Black-Scholes call gamma = phi(d1)/(S*sigma*sqrt(T)). Median gamma peaks near VEV_5200/5300 (~0.0022); wings ~1e-5. Same DTE mapping as live trader.
used_to_decide: Added gamma-based quote widening in trader_v8 (scale 0.15 vs GAMMA_REF from ATM medians). Backtest PnL tied v2 at 19255: sub-tick adjustment often does not change integer prices after rounding, so prefer simpler v2 unless we scale gamma bump in whole ticks (e.g. 1-2) in a follow-up.

analysis_description: Gamma bump tick-impact grid on days 0-2 (step 100): using sticky-smile fair_iv and tape-derived gamma, estimate additive half-width bump distribution across scales. Goal: ensure gamma term crosses integer tick thresholds after rounding.
used_to_decide: At scale 0.15, median bump=0.066 and 0% of samples >=0.5 tick (explains v8 tie). At scale 1.0, median bump=0.44 and 48% >=0.5 tick (non-trivial price impact), so tested v9. Backtest fell to 19147; conclusion: extra gamma-based widening is too conservative in this regime.

analysis_description: Smile c2 (quadratic IV curvature) variance: on tape days 0-2 step 100, per timestamp vega-weighted polyfit, then (A) one EWMA(0.045) of coeffs vs (B) same plus outer EWMA(0.12) of the result, matching trader_v10.
used_to_decide: Offline var(c2) is ~19% higher for the double-EMA path on this subsample (var_ratio ~1.19)—lag/phase can increase short-window coefficient volatility even while intent is 'smoother' fair. trader_v10 backtest still tied v2 (19255), so double layer is optional complexity; keep v2 as default unless a forward-looking smoother is justified.

analysis_description: Data-first cubic-spline wing stress: fit IV smile in logk with natural cubic spline and regularization lambda in {0,0.15,0.3} (blend to quadratic baseline), then evaluate wing extrapolation 20% outside observed logk support. Metrics: wing endpoint slopes, IQR of extrapolated IVs, negative-IV share. Additional run log and fill/activity summary recorded for quote quality under worse fills.
used_to_decide: Lambda=0.15 reduces left-wing slope and left extrapolation IQR vs no regularization with no negative-IV artifacts in stress. However v11 was too conservative in live quoting (0 non-zero VEV PnL products on all days), so quote quality/fill activity failed despite stable wings. Next cubic-spline iteration should loosen quoting aggressiveness while preserving wing-regularized spline.

analysis_description: Duplicate-check-driven variant shift: compare plain wing-regularized spline vs synthetic wing-anchor spline (stress 25% outside observed logk support). Metrics: wing endpoint slopes, extrapolated IV IQR, negative-IV share. Plus run log + fill summary for quote quality under worse fills.
used_to_decide: Anchor nodes reduced left-wing extrapolation IQR but increased right-wing slope/IQR in stress; backtest unchanged at 18,949 with zero VEV activity. Concludes wing stabilization alone is not the binding issue; next spline variant should focus on quote aggressiveness / edge gates while keeping regularization modest.

analysis_description: Priority analysis: underlying move propagation by contract (days 0-2). For each voucher, computed step elasticity dm/dS, BS delta at prior step IV, spread behavior under shock |dS|>=2, and lag corr (lag0/lag1) between extract returns and voucher returns. Added stress comparison plain vs wing-anchor spline extrapolation.
used_to_decide: Findings: strongest immediate response is core strikes (5000-5300) with high lag0 corr (~0.62-0.77) and near-zero lag1; wings are noisy or flat with very wide spreads (4000/4500) or tiny delta (6000/6500). Incorporated by using core-only shock-sensitive taking and tighter core quoting while leaving wings guarded.

analysis_description: Post-run quote quality check under worse fills for propagation-aware spline variants v13 and v14 using per-run logs and activity summaries.
used_to_decide: v13 activated VEVs but overtraded core down-moves (14923). v14 reduced adverse fills and improved to 18176, but still below v11/v12 and far below baseline v2. Conclusion: propagation logic directionally sensible for activity quality but current thresholds remain too aggressive for this simulator regime.

analysis_description: Prior work (spline_vs_poly_edge) showed cubic spline |mid-fair| much smaller than quadratic at quote points, explaining zero VEV fills for v11/v12. v15 implements a single design point in a small two-knob family: (1) blend weight w in {0.35} on BS(spline) vs BS(sticky poly from same node IVs), and (2) looser width floor 0.3*book vs 0.35*book in v12. Per-run backtest log + fill summary for activity check.
used_to_decide: Blend + tighter floor reactivated VEV trading on wing pair VEV_5400/VEV_4000 (no core fills) with net -19 on those VEVs over three days; total 18,968 vs 18,949 for v12 (+19) but still far from v2 (19,255). Suggests poly blend nudged quotes enough for occasional wing fills; next can sweep w in {0.2,0.35,0.5} and/or decouple width floor by strike (ATM vs wing) to limit wing adverse selection while seeking ATM activity.

analysis_description: Tape: instant cross-sectional poly vs wing-regularized spline |mid-BS| by contract (step 200, days 0-2, long-format CSV) — see spline_poly_edge_by_strike.json. Ratios (median |poly|/|spline|) are ~10x in 5000-5300 and similar elsewhere; 4000/4500/6500 also high but deep wings are illiquid (wide spread, low extract beta in underlying_propagation_by_strike.json). v16 first tried strike-specific w plus wider non-core quotes: backtest reverted to v12-like 18,949 with zero VEV (too wide for worse fills). Second v16: v15 width with w bucket grid — backtest 18,920, worse than v15 on wing 5400/4000 PnL.
used_to_decide: Conclude: lowering poly weight on illiquid wings while raising core w is not free — at integer tick resolution the wing bucket hurt more than core helped. Prefer keeping wing w at v15 unless paired with a different width rule.

analysis_description: Single-parameter sweep: only increase poly blend in 5000-5300 (w=0.40 vs 0.35) with wings unchanged, same as v15 otherwise — direct test of 'core needs more |mid-fair| from poly' without touching 5400/4000/6000 blend.
used_to_decide: Tie with v15 at 18,968 and identical 5400/4000 PnL pattern — extra core poly weight did not create new integer-tick quote states for 5000-5300. Next iterations should try width/edge on core only or a slightly larger w step (0.45) with caution, or accept v15 as local optimum for this blend family.

analysis_description: Tape: at each timestamp, flag extract shock |dS|>=2 vs prior mid within day; compare median bid1-ask1 spread for core VEV (5000-5300) vs deep wings (4000,4500,6500) on shock vs calm (analyze_extract_shock_spread_core_wing.py). Greeks thread unchanged from smile stack (IV from mid + BS vega in trader).
used_to_decide: Median core spread stays 3 on shocks vs calm; wings stay 16 either way — so shock is not a spread-widening regime for core books. Implemented v18: tiny core-only width/edge nudge only when |dS|>=2. Backtest is bit-for-bit same as v15 (18,968, same 5400/4000 lines): nudge is below integer tick threshold after round(fair±shift). Next shock-aware iteration needs either larger delta (risk adverse selection) or a non-width lever (e.g. core-only size bump without crossing into v13-style aggressive takes).

analysis_description: On extract shocks (|dS|>=2 vs prior timestamp, step-5 subsample for speed), compare median absolute change in voucher mid for core (5000-5300) vs near-wings (5400/5500) vs deep wings. Same IV/Greek stack as live trader for fair; this block is pure mid propagation stats.
used_to_decide: Core median |dm|=2 on up and down shocks; 5400/5500 median 0.5; deep wings 0 — supports core-only shock response. v19 adds +3 clip on core during shock; backtest identical to v15 (18,968): either no extra fills at same prices under worse matching, or core still not crossed. Larger boost or price-level change would be next if chasing core activity; avoid repeating v13 aggressive takes.
